author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Command name is /beads-design. Clean, descriptive, follows beads-* naming convention.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Architecture is delegation -- orchestrator calls existing /beads-brainstorm, /beads-plan, /beads-deepen, /beads-plan-review in sequence. Zero code duplication. Updates to individual commands automatically propagate.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Smart pauses -- interactive brainstorm phase, auto-run plan, pause to confirm plan before deepen, then auto-chain deepen + plan-review.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Phase gates with recovery -- each phase runs its existing verification. On failure, pause and offer retry/skip/abort.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Auto-apply safe feedback from plan-review. Only pause for trade-off decisions requiring user judgment.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Default to Comprehensive detail level (user can override). This command is for the full thorough pipeline.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: No skip flags. Auto-detect existing brainstorm context from bead ID. Keep the interface simple.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Follows /lfg precedent for compound commands that chain multiple steps.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Parallelism is preserved from existing commands -- each already maximally parallel internally (deepen spawns 20-40+ agents, plan-review runs 4 agents concurrently). Model hints (haiku/sonnet/opus) inherited.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Output style is progress banners at phase transitions + clear verification status per phase + final consolidated summary with epic bead ID, child bead count, decisions captured, and review findings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: npx smoke tests need conditional bd install step (same pattern as test-installation job) — bd not in CI by default

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: execFileSync with array args prevents shell injection entirely — use instead of execSync with template strings for any user-controlled input

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Command counts must be consistent across README, CATALOG, plugin.json description, pre-release-check.sh, and CI yaml — update all 5 locations when adding commands

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Extracted args.find() result to _positional variable to avoid double evaluation; paths starting with '-' remain unsupported (acceptable edge case for CLI usage)

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added exitCode parameter to usage() defaulting to 1; --help callers pass 0 explicitly — cleaner than duplicating print logic

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Math.max(0, rows - 3) prevents negative contentHeight; also replaced magic +20 scroll buffer with Math.max(contentHeight * 4, 80) so long descriptions scale with terminal size

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Always quote $GITHUB_WORKSPACE in shell steps — self-hosted runners may have spaces in workspace paths

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added --provenance and --access public to npm publish; added id-token: write permission for SLSA provenance attestation

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Already resolved in Wave 1 via _positional extraction in bdcompound-807.9 fix — single args.find() call

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Set timeout: 30000 (30s) to match plan-view.js pattern but longer since export is a one-shot operation not interactive

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Already resolved in Wave 1 — replaced contentHeight+20 with Math.max(contentHeight*4, 80) so buffer scales with terminal size

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Pinned to bun 1.3.6 (current installed version) and setup-bun@v2; this is a build-only tool so minor version drift risk is low but pinning is good hygiene

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed process.on('exit', cleanup) — all exit paths (SIGINT, SIGTERM, 'q' key) already call cleanup() explicitly before process.exit(); the handler was a redundant double-call

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Removing process.on('exit', cleanup) from a raw-mode TUI creates a terminal corruption class: any die() or unexpected exit path bypasses cleanup. Always keep an 'exit' event handler as the catch-all, not just SIGINT.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: continue-on-error: true on a conditionally-guarded CI step creates a double-silence: beads unavailable = skip, beads available but failing = also skip. Only use continue-on-error when you truly want silent failure; conditional guards are the right way to handle optional external dependencies.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When command counts change, update CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/plugin-catalog.md, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, README.md, plugin.json, pre-release-check.sh, and CI YAML (8 locations total). CLAUDE.md and plugin-catalog.md were missed this cycle.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Restored process.on('exit', cleanup) as defensive practice. Investigation showed die() calls all happen before setRawMode(true) in current code (buildTree runs at line 474, setRawMode at 487), so no current terminal corruption. However the handler is a zero-cost safety net for future code changes that might call die() after raw mode is active. Prior removal was based on correct analysis but removed a valuable defensive layer.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed continue-on-error: true — the conditional guard handles beads unavailability; continue-on-error on top was silencing real failures. Conditional guard = skip when unavailable. No continue-on-error = fail when available but broken.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Raised bdExecFile timeout from 10000ms to 30000ms to match plan-export.js. The failure mode in plan-view (silent empty string) is worse than plan-export (explicit error), so if anything plan-view needs the longer timeout more. Both tools call the same bd binary on the same data.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Updated OpenCode/Gemini thresholds in pre-release-check.sh from 25 to 29 to match CI verify-release job. Both files have SYNC: comments — they must always match. The Cortex threshold was left at 25 because CI does not check Cortex outputs in verify-release.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Updated command counts to 29 in CLAUDE.md (2 places), plugin-catalog.md (header + 3 missing entries added), and marketplace.json description. Also added beads-design, beads-quick, beads-import to plugin-catalog.md table.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used workflow_run trigger instead of needs: — cross-workflow dependencies require workflow_run in GitHub Actions. npm-publish now only fires when Test Installation workflow completes successfully AND the triggering event was a release.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced curl|bash with brew install beads on macos-latest runners. Homebrew is a package manager with version pinning, checksums, and signing — eliminates the supply chain risk entirely. Also eliminates the fragile availability check since brew install fails clearly if the package is unavailable. macos-latest runners ship with Homebrew pre-installed so no extra setup needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Moved validateBeadId to entry point only (after beadId is parsed, before any fetching begins). Removed redundant per-call-site guards — execFileSync already prevents injection, and the ID doesn't change between calls. Also fixed: requires grouped at top of plan-export.js, process.stderr.write -> console.error in plan-view.js.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added Cortex command count check (>= 29) to verify-release job to match pre-release-check.sh. Added --frozen-lockfile to bun install in verify-release for reproducibility. Both changes sync the CI gate with the local pre-release script.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with research from 7 agents (4 research, 3 review). Key improvements: (1) Config moved from .beads/memory/ to .beads/config/ due to merge=union corruption risk, (2) Bead .5 rescoped from filesystem scanning to config-based injection due to security/complexity concerns, (3) Review Context must use XML wrapping to prevent prompt injection, (4) Cross-check validation scoped to warnings-only with high-value checks

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Store config in .beads/config/project-setup.md not .beads/memory/. Reason: .beads/memory/ uses merge=union gitattributes which would corrupt YAML frontmatter during git merges. .beads/config/ provides clean separation (memory=knowledge, config=settings).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Dropped filesystem scanning of ~/.claude/agents/ and .claude/agents/ for dynamic agent discovery. Reasons: (1) CRITICAL security risk - arbitrary .md files injected into prompts, (2) ~/.claude/agents/ writable by any process including malicious npm postinstall, (3) YAGNI - no current user complaint. Rescoped to: read project-setup config and inject Review Context into subagent prompts.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: yaml-parser.ts line 15 has a bug: yamlLoad.CORE_SCHEMA is undefined (yamlLoad is a function, not an object). This causes silent fallback to DEFAULT_SCHEMA which allows !!js/function tags. Fix: import CORE_SCHEMA directly from js-yaml. This is a pre-existing bug, not part of this epic, but should be fixed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review (second pass) found 1 P1 critical (yaml-parser CORE_SCHEMA bug), 8 important suggestions, 4 minor. Key structural changes: merge .5 into .2, drop .6 or extract to orchestrator, simplify .1 (no profiles/monorepo/version), simplify .3 Sources (freeform list). Protected artifacts need 6 locations updated, not 1.
